functions.name: rtslib.utils.fwrite
rtslib.utils.fread
documentation:
This function reads the contents of a file.
It takes care of opening and closing it.

```pycon
>>> from rtslib.utils import *
>>> fwrite("/tmp/test", "hello")
>>> fread("/tmp/test")
'hello'
>>> fread("/tmp/notexistingfile") # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
IOError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/tmp/notexistingfile'
```

@param path: The path to the file to read from.
@type path: string
@return: A string containing the file's contents.

functions.name: rtslib.utils.is_dev_in_use
rtslib.utils._get_size_for_dev
rtslib.utils.get_size_for_blk_dev
rtslib.utils.get_size_for_disk_name
rtslib.utils.get_blockdev_type
documentation:
This function returns a block device's type.
Example: 0 is TYPE_DISK
If no match is found, None is returned.

```pycon
>>> from rtslib.utils import *
>>> get_blockdev_type("/dev/sda")
0
>>> get_blockdev_type("/dev/sr0")
5
>>> get_blockdev_type("/dev/scd0")
5
>>> get_blockdev_type("/dev/nodevicehere") is None
True
```

@param path: path to the block device
@type path: string
@return: An int for the block device type, or None if not a block device.

functions.name: rtslib.utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl
documentation:
This function returns the SCSI ID in H:C:T:L form for the block
device being mapped to the udev path specified.
If no match is found, None is returned.

```pycon
>>> import rtslib.utils as utils
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/scd0')
(2, 0, 0, 0)
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sr0')
(2, 0, 0, 0)
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sda')
(3, 0, 0, 0)
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sda1')
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sdb')
(3, 0, 1, 0)
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sdc')
(3, 0, 2, 0)
```

@param path: The udev path to the SCSI block device.
@type path: string
@return: An (host, controller, target, lun) tuple of integer
values representing the SCSI ID of the device, or raise RTSLibError.

functions.name: rtslib.utils.modprobe
rtslib.utils._get_auth_attr
documentation: These two functions are meant to be used with functools.partial and
properties.
'ignore=True' will silently return None if the attribute is not present.
This is good for attributes only present in some kernel versions.
All curried arguments should be keyword args.
These should only be used for attributes that follow the convention of
"NULL" having a special sentinel value, such as auth attributes, and
that return a string.

functions.name: rtslib.utils._set_auth_attr
documentation: Auth params take the string "NULL" to unset the attribute

documentation: Run the doctests

classes.name: rtslib.utils.RTSLibNotInCFS
documentation:
The underlying configfs object does not exist. Happens when
calling methods of an object that is instantiated but have
been deleted from congifs, or when trying to lookup an
object that does not exist.

modules.name: rtslib.fabric
documentation:
This file is part of RTSLib.
Copyright (c) 2011-2013 by Datera, Inc
Copyright (c) 2011-2014 by Red Hat, Inc.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may
not use this file except in compliance with the License. You may obtain
a copy of the License at

    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT
WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the
License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations
under the License.


Description
-----------

Fabrics may differ in how fabric WWNs are represented, as well as
what capabilities they support


Available parameters
--------------------

* features
Lists the target fabric available features. Default value:
("discovery_auth", "acls", "auth", "nps")
example: features = ("discovery_auth", "acls", "auth")
example: features = () # no features supported

Detail of features:

  * tpgts
  The target fabric module is using iSCSI-style target portal group tags.

  * discovery_auth
  The target fabric module supports a fabric-wide authentication for
  discovery.

  * acls
  The target's TPGTs support explicit initiator ACLs.

  * auth
  The target's TPGT's support per-TPG authentication, and
  the target's TPGT's ACLs support per-ACL initiator authentication.
  Fabrics that support auth must support acls.

  * nps
  The TPGTs support iSCSI-like IPv4/IPv6 network portals, using IP:PORT
  group names.

  * nexus
  The TPGTs have a 'nexus' attribute that contains the local initiator
  serial unit. This attribute must be set before being able to create any
  LUNs.

  * wwn_types
  Sets the type of WWN expected by the target fabric. Defaults to 'free'.
  Usually a fabric will only support one type but iSCSI supports more.
  First entry is the "native" wwn type - i.e. if a wwn can be generated, it
  will be of this type.
  Example: wwn_types = ("eui",)
  Current valid types are:

    * free
    Freeform WWN.

    * iqn
    The fabric module targets are using iSCSI-type IQNs.

    * naa
    NAA FC or SAS address type WWN.

    * eui
    EUI-64. See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MAC_address for info on this format.

    * unit_serial
    Disk-type unit serial.

* wwns
This property returns an iterable (either generator or list) of valid
target WWNs for the fabric, if WWNs should be chosen from existing
fabric interfaces. The most common case for this is hardware-set
WWNs. WWNs should conform to rtslib's normalized internal format: the wwn
type (see above), a period, then the wwn with interstitial dividers like
':' removed.

* to_fabric_wwn()
Converts WWNs from normalized format (see above) to whatever the kernel code
expects when getting a wwn. Only needed if different from normalized format.

* kernel_module
Sets the name of the kernel module implementing the fabric modules. If
not specified, it will be assumed to be MODNAME_target_mod, where
MODNAME is the name of the fabric module, from the fabrics list. Note
that you must not specify any .ko or such extension here.
Example: self.kernel_module = "my_module"

* _path
Sets the path of the configfs group used by the fabric module. Defaults to the
name of the module from the fabrics list.
Example: self._path = "%s/%s" % (self.configfs_dir, "my_cfs_dir")

classes.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule

methods.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule.__init__
documentation: FabricModule ABC private stuff

methods.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule._check_self
documentation: FabricModule public stuff

methods.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule.to_normalized_wwn
documentation:
Checks whether or not the provided WWN is valid for this fabric module
according to the spec, and returns a tuple of our preferred string 
representation of the wwn, and what type it turned out to be.

methods.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule.to_fabric_wwn
documentation:
Some fabrics need WWNs in a format different than rtslib's internal
format. These fabrics should override this method.

methods.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule.from_fabric_wwn
documentation:
Converts from WWN format used in this fabric's LIO configfs to canonical
format.
Note: Do not call from wwns(). There's no guarantee fabric wwn format is
the same as wherever wwns() is reading from.

methods.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule.needs_wwn
documentation:
This fabric requires wwn to be specified when creating a target,
it cannot be autogenerated.

methods.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule._get_wwns
documentation:
Returns either iterable or None. None means fabric allows
arbitrary WWNs.

methods.name: rtslib.fabric._BaseFabricModule.setup
documentation: Setup fabricmodule with settings from fm dict.

Abstract Base clase for Fabric Modules.
It can load modules, provide information about them and
handle the configfs housekeeping. After instantiation, whether or
not the fabric module is loaded depends on if a method requiring
it (i.e. accessing configfs) is used. This helps limit loaded
kernel modules to just the fabrics in use.

methods.name: rtslib.fabric.SBPFabricModule.wwns
documentation: return 1st local guid (is unique) so our share is named uniquely

classes.name: rtslib.fabric.FabricModule
documentation: Maintain compatibility with existing FabricModule(fabricname) usage
e.g. FabricModule('iscsi') returns an ISCSIFabricModule

documentation: StorageObject private stuff

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObject._config_pr_aptpl
documentation:
LIO actually *writes* pr aptpl info to the filesystem, so we
need to read it in and squirt it back into configfs when we configure
the storage object. BLEH.

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObject.so_from_path
documentation: Build a StorageObject of the correct type from a configfs path.

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObject._gen_attached_luns
documentation:
Fast scan of luns attached to a storage object. This is an order of
magnitude faster than using root.luns and matching path on them.

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObject._list_attached_luns
documentation: Generates all luns attached to a storage object.

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObject._list_alua_tpgs
documentation: Generate all ALUA groups attach to a storage object.

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObject._get_alua_supported
documentation: Children should override if the backend did not always support ALUA

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObject.delete
documentation:
Recursively deletes a StorageObject object.
This will delete all attached LUNs currently using the StorageObject
object, and then the StorageObject itself. The underlying file and
block storages will not be touched, but all ramdisk data will be lost.
StorageObject public stuff

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObject.is_configured
documentation: @return: True if the StorageObject is configured, else returns False

This is an interface to storage objects in configFS. A StorageObject is
identified by its backstore and its name.

classes.name: rtslib.tcm.PSCSIStorageObject

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.PSCSIStorageObject.__init__
documentation:
A PSCSIStorageObject can be instantiated in two ways:
    - B{Creation mode}: If I{dev} is specified, the underlying configFS
      object will be created with that parameter. No PSCSIStorageObject
      with the same I{name} can pre-exist in the parent PSCSIBackstore
      in that mode, or instantiation will fail.
    - B{Lookup mode}: If I{dev} is not set, then the PSCSIStorageObject
      will be bound to the existing configFS object in the parent
      PSCSIBackstore having the specified I{name}. The underlying
      configFS object must already exist in that mode, or instantiation
      will fail.

@param name: The name of the PSCSIStorageObject.
@type name: string
@param dev: You have two choices:
    - Use the SCSI id of the device: I{dev="H:C:T:L"}.
    - Use the path to the SCSI device: I{dev="/path/to/dev"}.
@type dev: string
@return: A PSCSIStorageObject object.
PSCSIStorageObject private stuff
An interface to configFS storage objects for pscsi backstore.

classes.name: rtslib.tcm.RDMCPStorageObject

methods.name: rtslib.tcm.RDMCPStorageObject.__init__
documentation: RDMCPStorageObject private stuff
An interface to configFS storage objects for rd_mcp backstore.

classes.name: rtslib.tcm.FileIOStorageObject

classes.name: rtslib.tcm.BlockStorageObject

classes.name: rtslib.tcm.StorageObjectFactory
documentation:
Create a storage object based on a given path.
Only works for file & block.

classes.name: rtslib.tcm._Backstore
documentation:
Backstore is needed as a level in the configfs hierarchy, but otherwise useless.
1:1 so:backstore.
Created by storageobject ctor before SO configfs entry.

methods.name: rtslib.node.CFSNode._create_in_cfs_ine
documentation:
Creates the configFS node if it does not already exist, depending on
the mode.
any -> makes sure it exists, also works if the node already does exist
lookup -> make sure it does NOT exist
create -> create the node which must not exist beforehand

methods.name: rtslib.node.CFSNode._list_files
rtslib.node.CFSNode.list_parameters

rtslib.node.CFSNode.get_attribute
documentation:
@param attribute: The attribute's name. It is case-sensitive.
@return: The named attribute's value, as a string.

methods.name: rtslib.node.CFSNode.set_parameter
rtslib.node.CFSNode.get_parameter
rtslib.node.CFSNode.delete
documentation:
If the underlying configFS object does not exist, this method does
nothing. If the underlying configFS object exists, this method attempts
to delete it.

functions.name: rtslib.root._test
documentation: Run the doctests.

classes.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot

methods.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot.__init__
documentation:
Instantiate an RTSRoot object. Basically checks for configfs setup and
base kernel modules (tcm)

methods.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot._get_saveconf
documentation:
Fetch the configuration of all the blocks and return conf with
updated storageObject info and its related target configuraion of
given storage object path

methods.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot.dump
documentation:
Returns a dict representing the complete state of the target
config, suitable for serialization/deserialization, and then
handing to restore().
RTSRoot public stuff

methods.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot.clear_existing
documentation: Remove entire current configuration.

methods.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot.restore
documentation:
Takes a dict generated by dump() and reconfigures the target to match.
Returns list of non-fatal errors that were encountered.
Will refuse to restore over an existing configuration unless clear_existing
    is True.

methods.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot.save_to_file
documentation:
Write the configuration in json format to a file.
Save file defaults to '/etc/target/saveconfig.json'.

methods.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot.restore_from_file
documentation:
Restore the configuration from a file in json format.
Restore file defaults to '/etc/target/saveconfig.json'.
Returns a list of non-fatal errors. If abort_on_error is set,
  it will raise the exception instead of continuing.

methods.name: rtslib.root.RTSRoot.invalidate_caches
documentation: Invalidate any caches used throughout the hierarchy

This is an interface to the root of the configFS object tree.
Is allows one to start browsing Target and StorageObjects,
as well as helper methods to return arbitrary objects from the
configFS tree.

```pycon
>>> import rtslib.root as root
>>> rtsroot = root.RTSRoot()
>>> rtsroot.path
'/sys/kernel/config/target'
>>> rtsroot.exists
True
>>> rtsroot.targets # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
[...]
>>> rtsroot.tpgs # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
[...]
>>> rtsroot.storage_objects # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
[...]
>>> rtsroot.network_portals # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
[...]
```

functions.name: rtslib_fb.utils.fwrite
rtslib_fb.utils.fread
documentation:
This function reads the contents of a file.
It takes care of opening and closing it.

```pycon
>>> from rtslib.utils import *
>>> fwrite("/tmp/test", "hello")
>>> fread("/tmp/test")
'hello'
>>> fread("/tmp/notexistingfile") # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
IOError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/tmp/notexistingfile'
```

@param path: The path to the file to read from.
@type path: string
@return: A string containing the file's contents.

documentation:
This function returns a block device's type.
Example: 0 is TYPE_DISK
If no match is found, None is returned.

```pycon
>>> from rtslib.utils import *
>>> get_blockdev_type("/dev/sda")
0
>>> get_blockdev_type("/dev/sr0")
5
>>> get_blockdev_type("/dev/scd0")
5
>>> get_blockdev_type("/dev/nodevicehere") is None
True
```

@param path: path to the block device

functions.name: rtslib_fb.utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl
documentation:
This function returns the SCSI ID in H:C:T:L form for the block
device being mapped to the udev path specified.
If no match is found, None is returned.

```pycon
>>> import rtslib.utils as utils
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/scd0')
(2, 0, 0, 0)
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sr0')
(2, 0, 0, 0)
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sda')
(3, 0, 0, 0)
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sda1')
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sdb')
(3, 0, 1, 0)
>>> utils.convert_scsi_path_to_hctl('/dev/sdc')
(3, 0, 2, 0)
```

@param path: The udev path to the SCSI block device.
@type path: string
@return: An (host, controller, target, lun) tuple of integer
values representing the SCSI ID of the device, or raise RTSLibError.

documentation:
The underlying configfs object does not exist. Happens when
calling methods of an object that is instantiated but have
been deleted from congifs, or when trying to lookup an
object that does not exist.

methods.name: rtslib.target.Target.__repr__
documentation: Target private stuff

methods.name: rtslib.target.Target.__init__
rtslib.target.Target.has_feature
documentation: Whether or not this Target has a certain feature.
Target public stuff

methods.name: rtslib.target.Target.delete
documentation:
Recursively deletes a Target object.
This will delete all attached TPG objects and then the Target itself.

methods.name: rtslib.target.Target.setup
documentation:
Set up target objects based upon t dict, from saved config.
Guard against missing or bad dict items, but keep going.
Call 'err_func' for each error.

This is an interface to Targets in configFS.
A Target is identified by its wwn.
To a Target is attached a list of TPG objects.

classes.name: rtslib.target.TPG

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG.__repr__
documentation: TPG private stuff

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG.__init__
rtslib.target.TPG._set_enable
documentation:
Enables or disables the TPG. If the TPG doesn't support the enable
attribute, do nothing.

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG._get_nexus
documentation: Gets the nexus initiator WWN, or None if the TPG does not have one.

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG._set_nexus
documentation:
Sets the nexus initiator WWN. Raises an exception if the nexus is
already set or if the TPG does not use a nexus.

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG.has_feature
documentation: Whether or not this TPG has a certain feature.
TPG public stuff

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG.delete
documentation:
Recursively deletes a TPG object.
This will delete all attached LUN, NetworkPortal and Node ACL objects
and then the TPG itself. Before starting the actual deletion process,
all sessions will be disconnected.

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG.node_acl
documentation: Same as NodeACL() but without specifying the parent_tpg.

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG.network_portal
documentation: Same as NetworkPortal() but without specifying the parent_tpg.

methods.name: rtslib.target.TPG.lun
documentation: Same as LUN() but without specifying the parent_tpg.

This is a an interface to Target Portal Groups in configFS.
A TPG is identified by its parent Target object and its TPG Tag.
To a TPG object is attached a list of NetworkPortals. Targets without
the 'tpgts' feature cannot have more than a single TPG, so attempts
to create more will raise an exception.

classes.name: rtslib.target.LUN

methods.name: rtslib.target.LUN.__repr__
documentation: LUN private stuff

methods.name: rtslib.target.LUN.__init__
rtslib.target.LUN._get_alua_tg_pt_gp_name
documentation: pass through backends will not have setup all the default
ALUA structs in the kernel. If the kernel has been setup,
a user created group or default_tg_pt_gp will be returned.
If the kernel was not properly setup an empty string is
return in alua_tg_pt_gp. Writing to alua_tg_pt_gp will crash
older kernels and will return a -Exyz code in newer ones.

methods.name: rtslib.target.LUN.delete
documentation:
If the underlying configFS object does not exist, this method does
nothing. If the underlying configFS object exists, this method attempts
to delete it along with all MappedLUN objects referencing that LUN.
LUN public stuff

This is an interface to RTS Target LUNs in configFS.
A LUN is identified by its parent TPG and LUN index.

classes.name: rtslib.target.NetworkPortal

methods.name: rtslib.target.NetworkPortal.__repr__
documentation: NetworkPortal private stuff

methods.name: rtslib.target.NetworkPortal.__init__
rtslib.target.NetworkPortal.delete
documentation: NetworkPortal public stuff

This is an interface to NetworkPortals in configFS.  A NetworkPortal is
identified by its IP and port, but here we also require the parent TPG, so
instance objects represent both the NetworkPortal and its association to a
TPG. This is necessary to get path information in order to create the
portal in the proper configFS hierarchy.

classes.name: rtslib.target.NodeACL

methods.name: rtslib.target.NodeACL.delete
documentation:
Delete the NodeACL, including all MappedLUN objects.
If the underlying configFS object does not exist, this method does
nothing.

methods.name: rtslib.target.NodeACL.mapped_lun
documentation: Same as MappedLUN() but without the parent_nodeacl parameter.

This is an interface to node ACLs in configFS.
A NodeACL is identified by the initiator node wwn and parent TPG.

classes.name: rtslib.target.MappedLUN

methods.name: rtslib.target.MappedLUN.__repr__
documentation: MappedLUN private stuff

methods.name: rtslib.target.MappedLUN.__init__
rtslib.target.MappedLUN.delete
documentation: Delete the MappedLUN.
MappedLUN public stuff

This is an interface to RTS Target Mapped LUNs.
A MappedLUN is a mapping of a TPG LUN to a specific initiator node, and is
part of a NodeACL. It allows the initiator to actually access the TPG LUN
if ACLs are enabled for the TPG. The initial TPG LUN will then be seen by
the initiator node as the MappedLUN.

classes.name: rtslib.target.Group

methods.name: rtslib.target.Group.__init__
documentation:
members_func is a function that takes a self argument
and returns an iterator of the objects that the
derived Group class is grouping.

methods.name: rtslib.target.Group._get_prop
documentation:
Helper fn to use with partial() to support getting a
property value from the first member of the group.
(All members of the group should be identical.)

methods.name: rtslib.target.Group._set_prop
documentation:
Helper fn to use with partial() to support setting a
property value in all members of the group.

Caution: Arguments reversed!
This is so partial() can be used on property name.

methods.name: rtslib.target.Group.delete
documentation: Delete all members of the group.

An abstract base class akin to CFSNode, but for classes that
emulate a higher-level group object across the actual NodeACL
configfs structure.

If wwn, set the password for just that wwn, otherwise set it for

If wwn, set the mutual_userid for just that wwn, otherwise set it for

Allow a group of NodeACLs that share a tag to be managed collectively.

modules.name: rtslib_fb.fabric
documentation:
This file is part of RTSLib.
Copyright (c) 2011-2013 by Datera, Inc
Copyright (c) 2011-2014 by Red Hat, Inc.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may
not use this file except in compliance with the License. You may obtain
a copy of the License at

    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT
WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the
License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations
under the License.


Description
-----------

Fabrics may differ in how fabric WWNs are represented, as well as
what capabilities they support


Available parameters
--------------------

* features
Lists the target fabric available features. Default value:
("discovery_auth", "acls", "auth", "nps")
example: features = ("discovery_auth", "acls", "auth")
example: features = () # no features supported

Detail of features:

  * tpgts
  The target fabric module is using iSCSI-style target portal group tags.

  * discovery_auth
  The target fabric module supports a fabric-wide authentication for
  discovery.

  * acls
  The target's TPGTs support explicit initiator ACLs.

  * auth
  The target's TPGT's support per-TPG authentication, and
  the target's TPGT's ACLs support per-ACL initiator authentication.
  Fabrics that support auth must support acls.

  * nps
  The TPGTs support iSCSI-like IPv4/IPv6 network portals, using IP:PORT
  group names.

  * nexus
  The TPGTs have a 'nexus' attribute that contains the local initiator
  serial unit. This attribute must be set before being able to create any
  LUNs.

  * wwn_types
  Sets the type of WWN expected by the target fabric. Defaults to 'free'.
  Usually a fabric will only support one type but iSCSI supports more.
  First entry is the "native" wwn type - i.e. if a wwn can be generated, it
  will be of this type.
  Example: wwn_types = ("eui",)
  Current valid types are:

    * free
    Freeform WWN.

    * iqn
    The fabric module targets are using iSCSI-type IQNs.

    * naa
    NAA FC or SAS address type WWN.

    * eui
    EUI-64. See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MAC_address for info on this format.

    * unit_serial
    Disk-type unit serial.

* wwns
This property returns an iterable (either generator or list) of valid
target WWNs for the fabric, if WWNs should be chosen from existing
fabric interfaces. The most common case for this is hardware-set
WWNs. WWNs should conform to rtslib's normalized internal format: the wwn
type (see above), a period, then the wwn with interstitial dividers like
':' removed.

* to_fabric_wwn()
Converts WWNs from normalized format (see above) to whatever the kernel code
expects when getting a wwn. Only needed if different from normalized format.

* kernel_module
Sets the name of the kernel module implementing the fabric modules. If
not specified, it will be assumed to be MODNAME_target_mod, where
MODNAME is the name of the fabric module, from the fabrics list. Note
that you must not specify any .ko or such extension here.
Example: self.kernel_module = "my_module"

* _path
Sets the path of the configfs group used by the fabric module. Defaults to the
name of the module from the fabrics list.
Example: self._path = "%s/%s" % (self.configfs_dir, "my_cfs_dir")

classes.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule.__init__
documentation: FabricModule ABC private stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule._check_self
documentation: FabricModule public stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule.to_normalized_wwn
documentation:
Checks whether or not the provided WWN is valid for this fabric module
according to the spec, and returns a tuple of our preferred string 
representation of the wwn, and what type it turned out to be.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule.to_fabric_wwn
documentation:
Some fabrics need WWNs in a format different than rtslib's internal
format. These fabrics should override this method.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule.from_fabric_wwn
documentation:
Converts from WWN format used in this fabric's LIO configfs to canonical
format.
Note: Do not call from wwns(). There's no guarantee fabric wwn format is
the same as wherever wwns() is reading from.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule.needs_wwn
documentation:
This fabric requires wwn to be specified when creating a target,
it cannot be autogenerated.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule._get_wwns
documentation:
Returns either iterable or None. None means fabric allows
arbitrary WWNs.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric._BaseFabricModule.setup
documentation: Setup fabricmodule with settings from fm dict.

Abstract Base clase for Fabric Modules.
It can load modules, provide information about them and
handle the configfs housekeeping. After instantiation, whether or
not the fabric module is loaded depends on if a method requiring
it (i.e. accessing configfs) is used. This helps limit loaded
kernel modules to just the fabrics in use.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.fabric.SBPFabricModule.wwns
documentation: return 1st local guid (is unique) so our share is named uniquely

classes.name: rtslib_fb.fabric.FabricModule
documentation: Maintain compatibility with existing FabricModule(fabricname) usage
e.g. FabricModule('iscsi') returns an ISCSIFabricModule

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.StorageObject._config_pr_aptpl
documentation:
LIO actually *writes* pr aptpl info to the filesystem, so we
need to read it in and squirt it back into configfs when we configure
the storage object. BLEH.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.StorageObject.so_from_path
documentation: Build a StorageObject of the correct type from a configfs path.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.StorageObject._gen_attached_luns
documentation:
Fast scan of luns attached to a storage object. This is an order of
magnitude faster than using root.luns and matching path on them.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.StorageObject._list_attached_luns
documentation: Generates all luns attached to a storage object.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.StorageObject._list_alua_tpgs
documentation: Generate all ALUA groups attach to a storage object.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.StorageObject._get_alua_supported
documentation: Children should override if the backend did not always support ALUA

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.StorageObject.delete
documentation:
Recursively deletes a StorageObject object.
This will delete all attached LUNs currently using the StorageObject
object, and then the StorageObject itself. The underlying file and
block storages will not be touched, but all ramdisk data will be lost.
StorageObject public stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.StorageObject.is_configured
documentation: @return: True if the StorageObject is configured, else returns False

This is an interface to storage objects in configFS. A StorageObject is
identified by its backstore and its name.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.PSCSIStorageObject

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.PSCSIStorageObject.__init__
documentation:
A PSCSIStorageObject can be instantiated in two ways:
    - B{Creation mode}: If I{dev} is specified, the underlying configFS
      object will be created with that parameter. No PSCSIStorageObject
      with the same I{name} can pre-exist in the parent PSCSIBackstore
      in that mode, or instantiation will fail.
    - B{Lookup mode}: If I{dev} is not set, then the PSCSIStorageObject
      will be bound to the existing configFS object in the parent
      PSCSIBackstore having the specified I{name}. The underlying
      configFS object must already exist in that mode, or instantiation
      will fail.

@param name: The name of the PSCSIStorageObject.
@type name: string
@param dev: You have two choices:
    - Use the SCSI id of the device: I{dev="H:C:T:L"}.
    - Use the path to the SCSI device: I{dev="/path/to/dev"}.
@type dev: string
@return: A PSCSIStorageObject object.
PSCSIStorageObject private stuff
An interface to configFS storage objects for pscsi backstore.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.tcm.RDMCPStorageObject.__init__

functions.name: rtslib_fb.root._test
documentation: Run the doctests.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot

methods.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot.__init__
documentation:
Instantiate an RTSRoot object. Basically checks for configfs setup and
base kernel modules (tcm)

methods.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot._get_saveconf
documentation:
Fetch the configuration of all the blocks and return conf with
updated storageObject info and its related target configuraion of
given storage object path

methods.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot.dump
documentation:
Returns a dict representing the complete state of the target
config, suitable for serialization/deserialization, and then
handing to restore().
RTSRoot public stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot.clear_existing
documentation: Remove entire current configuration.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot.restore
documentation:
Takes a dict generated by dump() and reconfigures the target to match.
Returns list of non-fatal errors that were encountered.
Will refuse to restore over an existing configuration unless clear_existing
    is True.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot.save_to_file
documentation:
Write the configuration in json format to a file.
Save file defaults to '/etc/target/saveconfig.json'.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot.restore_from_file
documentation:
Restore the configuration from a file in json format.
Restore file defaults to '/etc/target/saveconfig.json'.
Returns a list of non-fatal errors. If abort_on_error is set,
  it will raise the exception instead of continuing.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.root.RTSRoot.invalidate_caches
documentation: Invalidate any caches used throughout the hierarchy

This is an interface to the root of the configFS object tree.
Is allows one to start browsing Target and StorageObjects,
as well as helper methods to return arbitrary objects from the
configFS tree.

```pycon
>>> import rtslib.root as root
>>> rtsroot = root.RTSRoot()
>>> rtsroot.path
'/sys/kernel/config/target'
>>> rtsroot.exists
True
>>> rtsroot.targets # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
[...]
>>> rtsroot.tpgs # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
[...]
>>> rtsroot.storage_objects # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
[...]
>>> rtsroot.network_portals # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
[...]
```

classes.name: rtslib_fb.target.Target

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.Target.__repr__
documentation: Target private stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.Target.__init__
rtslib_fb.target.Target.has_feature
documentation: Whether or not this Target has a certain feature.
Target public stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.Target.delete
documentation:
Recursively deletes a Target object.
This will delete all attached TPG objects and then the Target itself.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.Target.setup
documentation:
Set up target objects based upon t dict, from saved config.
Guard against missing or bad dict items, but keep going.
Call 'err_func' for each error.

This is an interface to Targets in configFS.
A Target is identified by its wwn.
To a Target is attached a list of TPG objects.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG.__repr__
documentation: TPG private stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG.__init__
rtslib_fb.target.TPG._set_enable
documentation:
Enables or disables the TPG. If the TPG doesn't support the enable
attribute, do nothing.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG._get_nexus
documentation: Gets the nexus initiator WWN, or None if the TPG does not have one.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG._set_nexus
documentation:
Sets the nexus initiator WWN. Raises an exception if the nexus is
already set or if the TPG does not use a nexus.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG.has_feature
documentation: Whether or not this TPG has a certain feature.
TPG public stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG.delete
documentation:
Recursively deletes a TPG object.
This will delete all attached LUN, NetworkPortal and Node ACL objects
and then the TPG itself. Before starting the actual deletion process,
all sessions will be disconnected.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG.node_acl
documentation: Same as NodeACL() but without specifying the parent_tpg.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG.network_portal
documentation: Same as NetworkPortal() but without specifying the parent_tpg.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.TPG.lun
documentation: Same as LUN() but without specifying the parent_tpg.

This is a an interface to Target Portal Groups in configFS.
A TPG is identified by its parent Target object and its TPG Tag.
To a TPG object is attached a list of NetworkPortals. Targets without
the 'tpgts' feature cannot have more than a single TPG, so attempts
to create more will raise an exception.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.target.LUN

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.LUN.__repr__
documentation: LUN private stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.LUN.__init__
rtslib_fb.target.LUN._get_alua_tg_pt_gp_name
documentation: pass through backends will not have setup all the default
ALUA structs in the kernel. If the kernel has been setup,
a user created group or default_tg_pt_gp will be returned.
If the kernel was not properly setup an empty string is
return in alua_tg_pt_gp. Writing to alua_tg_pt_gp will crash
older kernels and will return a -Exyz code in newer ones.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.LUN.delete
documentation:
If the underlying configFS object does not exist, this method does
nothing. If the underlying configFS object exists, this method attempts
to delete it along with all MappedLUN objects referencing that LUN.
LUN public stuff

This is an interface to RTS Target LUNs in configFS.
A LUN is identified by its parent TPG and LUN index.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.target.NetworkPortal

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NetworkPortal.__repr__
documentation: NetworkPortal private stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NetworkPortal.__init__
rtslib_fb.target.NetworkPortal.delete
documentation: NetworkPortal public stuff

This is an interface to NetworkPortals in configFS.  A NetworkPortal is
identified by its IP and port, but here we also require the parent TPG, so
instance objects represent both the NetworkPortal and its association to a
TPG. This is necessary to get path information in order to create the
portal in the proper configFS hierarchy.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACL

documentation: NodeACL private stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACL.__init__
rtslib_fb.target.NodeACL.has_feature
documentation: Whether or not this NodeACL has a certain feature.
NodeACL public stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACL.delete
documentation:
Delete the NodeACL, including all MappedLUN objects.
If the underlying configFS object does not exist, this method does
nothing.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACL.mapped_lun
documentation: Same as MappedLUN() but without the parent_nodeacl parameter.

This is an interface to node ACLs in configFS.
A NodeACL is identified by the initiator node wwn and parent TPG.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.target.MappedLUN

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.MappedLUN.__repr__
documentation: MappedLUN private stuff

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.MappedLUN.__init__
rtslib_fb.target.MappedLUN.delete
documentation: Delete the MappedLUN.
MappedLUN public stuff

This is an interface to RTS Target Mapped LUNs.
A MappedLUN is a mapping of a TPG LUN to a specific initiator node, and is
part of a NodeACL. It allows the initiator to actually access the TPG LUN
if ACLs are enabled for the TPG. The initial TPG LUN will then be seen by
the initiator node as the MappedLUN.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.target.Group

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.Group.__init__
documentation:
members_func is a function that takes a self argument
and returns an iterator of the objects that the
derived Group class is grouping.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.Group._get_prop
documentation:
Helper fn to use with partial() to support getting a
property value from the first member of the group.
(All members of the group should be identical.)

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.Group._set_prop
documentation:
Helper fn to use with partial() to support setting a
property value in all members of the group.

Caution: Arguments reversed!
This is so partial() can be used on property name.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.Group.delete
documentation: Delete all members of the group.

An abstract base class akin to CFSNode, but for classes that
emulate a higher-level group object across the actual NodeACL
configfs structure.

rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.remove_acl

documentation: Gives access to the underlying NodeACLs within this group.

documentation: Give the Node WWNs of members of this group.

documentation: Whether or not this NodeACL has a certain feature.

documentation: Yields any current sessions.

documentation: Add a mapped lun to all group members.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.mapped_lun_groups
documentation: Generates all MappedLUNGroup objects in this NodeACLGroup.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.get_userids
documentation: Returns a (wwn, userid) tuple for each member of the group.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.set_userids
documentation:
If wwn, set the userid for just that wwn, otherwise set it for
all group members.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.get_passwords
documentation: Returns a (wwn, password) tuple for each member of the group.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.set_passwords
documentation:
If wwn, set the password for just that wwn, otherwise set it for
all group members.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.get_mutual_userids
documentation: Returns a (wwn, mutual_userid) tuple for each member of the group.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.set_mutual_userids
documentation:
If wwn, set the mutual_userid for just that wwn, otherwise set it for
all group members.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.get_mutual_passwords
documentation: Returns a (wwn, mutual_password) tuple for each member of the group.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.NodeACLGroup.set_mutual_passwords
documentation:
If wwn, set the mutual_password for just that wwn, otherwise set it for
all group members.
Allow a group of NodeACLs that share a tag to be managed collectively.

classes.name: rtslib_fb.target.MappedLUNGroup

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.MappedLUNGroup.mapped_lun
documentation: Get the integer MappedLUN mapped_lun index.

methods.name: rtslib_fb.target.MappedLUNGroup.parent_nodeaclgroup
documentation: Get the parent NodeACLGroup object.

Used with NodeACLGroup, this aggregates all MappedLUNs with the same LUN
so that it can be configured across all members of the NodeACLGroup.
